stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: The Hong Kong Polytechnic University Consulting Team
stakeholders.Role: Conducted a consultancy study on the needs and support required of informal carers of elderly persons and persons with disabilities in Hong Kong
Commissioned the consultancy study on carer support needs
Involved in the administration of carer-related subsidies and policies
Provided insights and evaluations on carer services and subsidies during the study

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Carers of elderly persons and persons with disabilities
stakeholders.Canonical Name: Carers of Elderly Persons and Persons with Disabilities
stakeholders.Role: Primary recipients of support and services evaluated in the consultancy study
Provided additional support services for carers such as training and counseling
Facilitated services for carers and recipients, enhancing access and coordination

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Stakeholders in the elderly and rehabilitation sectors
stakeholders.Role: Participated in focus groups and provided feedback on the needs and expectations of carers
Regulates and provides support for informal unpaid carers in Hong Kong

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Hong Kong Federation of Women’s Centres
stakeholders.Canonical Name: Hong Kong Federation of Women’s Centres
stakeholders.Role: Provides information and support services for carers
Facilitates carer support through various policies and initiatives
Offers community resources and support for caregivers and care recipients
Provides professional guidance and support to carers
Regulates elderly abuse prevention and intervention procedures.
Responsible for investigating elder abuse cases.
Provides medical care and mental health services for elder abuse victims.
Offers psychological support and treatment for elderly victims of abuse.
Provides support services for elderly to prevent abuse.
Introduces targeted employment support programmes for elderly workers
Introduces trial employment incentives for middle-aged and older workers
Conducts territory-wide Population Health Surveys
Analyzes elderly employment trends and policies
Raises retirement age for new recruits from 60 to 65
Offers employment programmes and training allowances for elderly job seekers
Implements policies and acts to improve elderly employment
Provides policy recommendations on ageing and employment
